us-politics.headline: US Jets Downed Over Iran as War Escalates
us-politics.body:
Two US warplanes were shot down over Iran, with at least one crew member still missing as recovery efforts continue. Two helicopters were also hit during search operations, raising the human and military cost of the conflict sharply. One Republican senator, John Curtis, has called for ending offensive operations without a formal declaration of war, and Rep. Nancy Mace voiced concern about escalation and the prospect of ground troops.

Bipartisan unease is growing on Capitol Hill. Republicans who typically back the administration are pushing back, while Democrats are criticising the White House's wartime communication. NPR analysis notes Trump's rhetoric differs markedly from past wartime presidents, with analysts warning the gap between language and legal process carries real risks.

sources.title: What to know about US fighter jets hit in Iran war
2 US helicopters hit during recovery efforts in Iran
GOP senator calls to end Iran operations without formal declaration of war
Two U.S. warplanes shot down; search ongoing in Iran for 1 missing crew member
Mace ‘concerned about escalation’ in Iran war
Democrat rips Polymarket for taking bets on fate of US pilot shot down over Iran
How Trump's wartime rhetoric differs from past presidents and why that matters

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Draws Fire From Within GOP
us-politics.body:
Trump's budget proposal includes a $10 billion fund for Washington DC construction and a major defence spending outline, but it is already facing resistance from unexpected corners. Republican Senator Susan Collins publicly criticised funding cuts in the White House proposal, a rare break that signals the budget faces a difficult path through the Senate.

Todd Blanche has taken over as acting Attorney General following Pam Bondi's departure, stepping into a role carrying heavy expectations from Trump. Separately, the Washington Post reports Trump is weighing further Cabinet changes while trying to avoid what aides describe as a disruptive overhaul. Blanche told reporters there is no evidence in the Epstein files that Epstein worked as a spy.

sources.title: Trump weighs more Cabinet changes, but wants to avoid ‘massive shake-up’
Todd Blanche takes on DOJ’s top job and the burden of Trump’s expectations
Susan Collins knocks funding cuts in White House budget proposal
What to know about Trump’s defense budget proposal
Blanche: No evidence in Epstein files he was a spy
Trump proposes $10B fund for D.C. construction, beautification projects

us-politics.headline: States Sue Over Trump Mail-In Voting Order
us-politics.body:
Multiple Democratic-led states filed lawsuits challenging Trump's executive order restricting vote-by-mail, with Oregon's attorney general leading one action and describing the order as creating "confusion, chaos and distrust." The legal challenges argue the order oversteps federal authority over state election administration.

The fight over mail voting is shaping up as one of the sharpest electoral battlegrounds of Trump's second term. Democrats frame the restrictions as voter suppression; supporters argue tighter rules protect election integrity. Courts will likely determine the outcome before the 2026 midterms.

sources.title: ‘Confusion, chaos and distrust’: Oregon challenges Trump’s order restricting vote-by-mail
Democratic-led states sue over Trump mail-in ballot order

us-politics.headline: Trump Signs College Sports Order; Senate Tensions Simmer
us-politics.body:
Trump signed an executive order targeting college sports reform, directing changes to athlete compensation and governance structures that have been in flux since the NCAA's name, image and likeness rules took effect. The order drew mixed reactions, with supporters calling it overdue federal intervention and critics questioning whether the White House has clear authority over collegiate athletics.

Elsewhere on Capitol Hill, Senator Thom Tillis set a firm condition on the attorney general nominee tied to January 6, while Senate Democrats pressed the Treasury Department over reports that Trump's name may appear on US currency. GOP lawmakers also rallied around the Army chief of staff ousted by Defence Secretary Hegseth, adding another friction point between Congress and the Pentagon.

sources.title: Trump signs order aimed at reforming college sports
Trump again orders changes to ‘save’ college sports
Trump gives rare praise to persistent GOP foe after White House ballroom vote
Senate Democrats press Treasury Department on decision to add Trump name to currency
Tillis sets Jan. 6 red line on attorney general nominee
GOP praise pours in for Army chief of staff ousted by Hegseth

au-politics.headline: Pezzullo Report Finds Former Home Affairs Boss 'Reckless'
au-politics.body:
A confidential report has found former Home Affairs secretary Michael Pezzullo acted recklessly in his dealings with a Liberal powerbroker, adding fresh detail to one of Australia's most scrutinised public service scandals. Pezzullo was sacked in 2023 after an inquiry found he breached the public service code of conduct through years of political messaging with Liberal operative Scott Briggs.

The new findings suggest the conduct was more serious than previously disclosed. Critics argue the case exposes deep problems with political entanglement at the top of the public service. Supporters of Pezzullo have previously contended the punishment was disproportionate and politically motivated. The report has not been released publicly.

sources.title: Confidential report found former home affairs boss Michael Pezzullo was ‘reckless’ in engagement with Liberal powerbroker

au-politics.headline: Fuel Supply Fears Drive Panic Buying Despite Strong Stocks
au-politics.body:
Energy Minister Chris Bowen says Australia's fuel supplies remain strong, with fewer service stations running dry compared to recent weeks. Despite that reassurance, panic buying has kept demand elevated, and farmers are now calling on supermarkets to absorb higher transport costs rather than passing them down the supply chain.

The tension exposes a fault line between retail giants and agricultural producers already under pressure. The Spectator has separately criticised government messaging around fuel security, arguing tyre-pressure advice offered to motorists reflects a broader pattern of deflecting responsibility. The government maintains the situation is stabilising, but the gap between official confidence and public behaviour remains wide.

sources.title: Fewer service stations running out of petrol and diesel as Australia’s fuel supplies remain strong, energy minister says
Supermarkets asked to pay more for produce to absorb fuel costs for farmers
Just, you know, check your tyre pressure

au-politics.headline: Nine Charged Over Alleged Cocaine and Meth 'Mother Ship' Plot
au-politics.body:
Nine people face charges over an alleged conspiracy to import large quantities of cocaine and methamphetamine into Australia using a vessel operating as a so-called mother ship in Australian waters. The operation unravelled after a trawler sank, alerting police to the alleged smuggling network.

Victoria Police made arrests following a major investigation. Authorities say the syndicate had the capacity to move tonnes of narcotics in a single operation. The case highlights ongoing pressure on Australian law enforcement to intercept maritime drug trafficking, which agencies have repeatedly identified as a growing threat to the country's borders.

sources.title: Nine charged over alleged conspiracy to import tonnes of cocaine and meth via ‘mother ship’ in Australian waters
Sunken trawler alerts police to alleged drug-smuggling plan

au-politics.headline: Victorian Election Takes Shape as Challenger Targets Premier Allan
au-politics.body:
Andrew Lethlean has announced he will contest the seat of Bendigo East against Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan at the next state election, setting up a direct challenge to the state's top political office. Lethlean's candidacy signals the opposition is targeting Allan's own electorate as a pressure point.

Allan has held Bendigo East since 2002 and became Premier in 2023 following Daniel Andrews' resignation. A successful challenge would be a major political upset. The move reflects broader opposition strategy to contest Labor in regional Victoria, where cost-of-living pressures and infrastructure disputes have tested the government's standing outside Melbourne.

sources.title: Lethlean to challenge Victorian premier in Bendigo East

world.headline: US-Israeli War on Iran Escalates as Missiles Hit Israel
world.body:
Now in its 36th day, the US-Israeli military campaign against Iran is intensifying on multiple fronts. Tehran claimed to have shot down two American warplanes, while Iranian missiles struck residential areas in central Israel. Israel simultaneously bombed bridges in Lebanon, widening the conflict's geographic reach.

The Strait of Hormuz remains a flashpoint, with questions mounting over whether force will be used to reopen the critical shipping lane. A rescue team inside Iran is conducting what officials describe as a harrowing search for a missing US crew member, underscoring how deeply the conflict has drawn in American personnel on the ground.

sources.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 36 of US-Israeli attacks?
Iranian missile attack hits residential areas in central Israel
Rescue team in Iran face 'harrowing and dangerous' search for US crew member
Iran war live: Tehran downs 2 US warplanes; Israel bombs Lebanon bridges
Will force be used to reopen Strait of Hormuz?
Survivors of Iran bridge attack recall terror of US-Israeli strike

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Halfway to Moon, Sends Back Earth Images
world.body:
NASA's Artemis II crew has passed the halfway point of their lunar journey, releasing striking photographs of Earth that have drawn widespread attention. The images, described as spectacular, mark the first time humans have photographed Earth from this distance since the Apollo era.

The mission represents a major milestone in NASA's broader push to return astronauts to the Moon. The crew remains on course, with the images serving as both a scientific record and a rare moment of shared wonder amid an otherwise turbulent global news cycle.

sources.title: Artemis II crew now halfway to Moon as they take 'spectacular' image of Earth
NASA unveils first images of Earth from Artemis II
NASA releases stunning first photos of Earth from Artemis II moon mission

world.headline: Cuba Frees 2,000 Prisoners as US Pressure Bites
world.body:
Cuba has begun releasing more than 2,000 prisoners, a significant concession that follows sustained pressure from Washington. The move signals a rare shift from Havana, which has historically resisted external demands on its domestic policy.

The releases come as the Trump administration maintains an aggressive posture toward the island. Whether the prisoner releases translate into broader diplomatic movement remains unclear, but the scale of the action suggests Cuba is responding to economic and political leverage rather than acting from goodwill.

sources.title: Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners amid mounting US pressure

world.headline: Trump Drilling Push, Democrat Lawsuits, and Powell Ruling
world.body:
The Trump administration opened protected wilderness areas to oil and gas development, advancing its "drill baby drill" agenda and reversing conservation-era restrictions on federal land. Environmental groups condemned the move, while energy companies welcomed expanded access.

Separately, two dozen Democrat-led states filed suit against the administration over restrictions on mail-in voting, escalating legal battles over election rules ahead of future cycles. A federal judge also upheld a decision to discard subpoenas targeting Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, blocking an attempt to compel testimony from the central bank's leadership.

sources.title: 'Drill baby drill': Trump opens wilderness to big energy
Two dozen Democrat-led states sue Trump over mail-in ballot limits
US judge upholds decision to toss subpoenas into Fed Chair Jerome Powell

ai.headline: Anthropic Expands Into Biotech, Politics, and Private Markets
ai.body:
Anthropic is moving fast on multiple fronts. The AI company reportedly acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, launched a political action committee to shape AI policy in Washington, and is drawing intense investor attention in private markets - though analysts warn SpaceX's anticipated IPO could siphon capital away.

On the research side, Anthropic published findings on how emotion concepts function inside large language models, adding scientific weight to its safety-focused brand. The company also drew controversy by restricting Claude Code subscribers from using OpenClaw, a third-party integration, effectively forcing users to pay extra or abandon the tool. Security researchers separately flagged OpenClaw as a compromise risk, giving Anthropic's move some cover - though critics called it an anticompetitive squeeze.

sources.title: Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model
Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw
OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC

ai.headline: OpenAI Reshuffles Leadership as Key Executives Step Back
ai.body:
OpenAI is reorganizing at the top. COO Brad Lightcap is moving into a new 'special projects' role, while Fidji Simo, who joined less than a year ago to lead the consumer business, is taking medical leave. The head of OpenAI's AGI division is also stepping back.

The simultaneous departures raise questions about internal stability at a company already navigating a fraught transition from nonprofit to for-profit structure. No single successor has been named to cover the gaps, and the timing - amid fierce competition from Anthropic and Google - makes the shuffle harder to dismiss as routine.

sources.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up

ai.headline: Musk Ties SpaceX IPO Banks to Mandatory Grok Subscriptions
ai.body:
Elon Musk is reportedly requiring banks competing for roles on SpaceX's IPO to purchase subscriptions to Grok, his xAI chatbot product. The demand puts financial institutions in an awkward position - pay up or risk losing a marquee deal.

The tactic fits a pattern of Musk leveraging his business empire to cross-promote properties, but attaching product purchases to investment banking mandates is unusual enough to draw regulatory scrutiny. SpaceX remains one of the most valuable private companies in the world, giving Musk significant leverage over banks eager for the business.

sources.title: Elon Musk insists banks working on SpaceX IPO must buy Grok subscriptions

ai.headline: AI Erodes Critical Thinking as Data Center Costs Mount
ai.body:
New research finds that heavy AI users increasingly defer to model outputs without applying independent judgment - a pattern researchers are calling 'cognitive surrender.' The findings suggest that convenience is quietly reshaping how people reason, not just what they produce.

Meanwhile, the infrastructure powering these tools carries its own costs. AI companies are building large natural gas plants to meet surging data center demand, drawing environmental criticism. Trump's push for a domestic AI data center buildout is also stalling, with analysts pointing to his own tariff policies as a key obstacle - raising the price of the hardware needed to make the buildout happen.

sources.title: "Cognitive surrender" leads AI users to abandon logical thinking, research finds
Trump ignores biggest reasons his AI data center buildout is failing
AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?

btc.headline: Bitcoin Consolidates as ETF Dominance Over Gold Looks Inevitable
btc.body:
Bitcoin is grinding sideways, and analysts say the tighter the range gets, the harder the eventual break. Consolidation without clear direction often precedes sharp moves in either direction, making the current price action a coiled spring rather than a dead market.

Meanwhile, the longer-term bull case keeps building. Bloomberg analyst James Seyffart argues Bitcoin ETFs will ultimately surpass gold ETFs in size, pointing to Bitcoin's expanding use cases and the structural demand that spot ETF approval unlocked. Gold ETFs took decades to reach their current scale. Bitcoin is moving faster.

sources.title: Bitcoin’s ‘no direction’ action may lead to heavier breakout: Analyst
Bitcoin ETFs 'will be larger' than gold ETFs: Analyst

btc.headline: Quantum Threat to Bitcoin Is Real but Distant
btc.body:
Headlines claiming quantum computers can crack Bitcoin in nine minutes are misleading. The research describes breaking a simplified cryptographic key under ideal lab conditions - not attacking the live Bitcoin network, which operates at a scale orders of magnitude more complex.

The threat is real over a longer horizon, and the crypto industry knows it. Post-quantum cryptography research is active, and Bitcoin developers have time to adapt. The danger is complacency, not imminent collapse.

sources.title: Here's what 'cracking' bitcoin in 9 minutes by quantum computers actually means

btc.headline: Ethereum Foundation Hits Staking Milestone as Cambodia Cracks Down
btc.body:
The Ethereum Foundation is closing in on its 70,000 staked ETH target, a move that signals stronger institutional commitment to the network's proof-of-stake model and puts the Foundation's own skin in the game alongside validators.

Elsewhere in regulation, Cambodia is pushing legislation that would impose severe prison sentences on crypto scammers. The proposal reflects a broader regional shift toward treating crypto fraud as serious financial crime rather than a grey-area enforcement problem.

sources.title: Ethereum Foundation nearly reaches 70,000 staked ETH goal
Cambodian lawmakers propose severe prison time for crypto scammers

btc.headline: Nevada Court Keeps Kalshi Sports Markets Blocked
btc.body:
A Nevada judge extended the ban on Kalshi's sports prediction markets, dealing another setback to the platform's push into regulated event contracts. The ruling keeps Kalshi out of one of the country's most active sports betting states while the legal fight continues.

The case sits at the intersection of prediction markets and traditional gambling law - a boundary regulators and courts are still drawing. The outcome will shape how platforms like Kalshi can operate across state lines.

sources.title: Judge continues Nevada ban on Kalshi sports markets

food.name: Miso-Glazed Leek and Walnut Tart
food.description: A buttery shortcrust tart filled with slow-caramelised leeks, toasted walnuts, and a white miso custard that balances umami with subtle sweetness.
food.tip: Blind-bake the pastry shell for 12 minutes before adding the filling — miso custard is wetter than it looks and a soggy base will ruin the whole thing.

exercise.name: The Saturday Ladder
exercise.equipment: A single kettlebell (or a heavy backpack as substitute)
exercise.description: A ascending-then-descending rep ladder — swings, goblet squats, and single-arm rows — starting at 3 reps per move and climbing to 10 before coming back down, with no fixed rest periods, just honest breathing breaks.

learn.description: Stochastic resonance is the counterintuitive phenomenon where adding a precise amount of random noise to a weak signal actually makes that signal easier to detect — exploited by everything from crayfish nervous systems to medical imaging devices.
learn.why: It quietly dismantles the assumption that noise is always the enemy of clarity, and once you understand it you start spotting its fingerprints in neuroscience, economics, and signal processing — a genuinely rewarding Saturday rabbit hole.
